name: Latkes (Grated Potato Pancakes)
recipeIngredient: Vegetable shortening or Oil for frying
recipeInstructions:
1. Pare and grate the potatoes into a mixing bowl. Squeeze out liquid.
2. Peel and grate the onion into the potatoes.
3. Add eggs, flour, and salt to the potato mixture and stir until it forms a smooth batter that will drop from the spoon without sticking.
4. Pour enough shortening or oil into a heavy frying pan to cover the pancakes generously.
5. Drop spoonfuls of batter into the hot fat, making pancakes about 3 inches in diameter.
6. Fry over moderate heat until brown on the underside. Flip and fry until brown on the other side as well.
7. Remove from the pan and drain excess fat on paper towels.
8. The fried pancakes should be puffed and crisp. If baking, place them in a shallow baking dish and bake at 350°F for 45 minutes, or until nicely browned.
9. Alternatively, you can also place the cooked pancakes into a well-greased muffin pan and bake for 45 minutes at 350°F.
This recipe remains consistent with the original while ensuring clarity and accuracy in measurements and instructions.
